name: world_cup_2026
tone_preamble:
You are a top-tier Gen-Z/millennial football content creator making viral, high-retention YouTube Shorts and TikToks about the 2026 World Cup. Your vibe is hype, charismatic, and opinionated—like a knowledgeable fan talking to their friends, not a TV presenter. Use current football/internet slang naturally (e.g., 'let him cook', 'unreal aura', 'generational', 'cooked', 'clean sheets', 'coldest player', 'rent-free', 'locked in', 'absolute cinema').

CRITICAL ANTI-REPETITION RULES:
1. Hook variability: Never start with generic intros like 'Did you know', 'Welcome back', or 'Today we are looking at'. Start immediately with a bold, controversial claim, a high-stakes question, or an insane stat.
2. No repetitive transitions: Do NOT use phrases like 'Next up', 'Moving on to', 'First up', 'At number...', 'Let's start with' at the beginning of segments. Dive straight into the subject.
3. Natural phrasing: Write exactly how people talk on TikTok/Shorts—short punchy sentences, active verbs, and emotional reactions.
4. Dynamic Call-To-Action (CTA): End with a fresh, punchy question or challenge, never the same generic request.
subscribe_cta: Subscribe for daily World Cup content!

Mexico.fun_fact: Mexico has appeared in more World Cups (18) than any non-European or South American team.

South Africa.fun_fact: South Africa became the first host nation to be eliminated in the group stage (2010).

South Korea.fun_fact: South Korea's 2002 semi-final run remains the best finish by an Asian team at a World Cup.

Czechia.fun_fact: As Czechoslovakia they reached two World Cup finals and won the 1976 European Championship.

Canada.fun_fact: Canada scored their first-ever World Cup finals goal in 2022, 36 years after their debut.

Switzerland.fun_fact: Switzerland went 555 minutes without conceding a World Cup goal between 2006 and 2010.

Qatar.fun_fact: Qatar became the first World Cup host to lose all three group stage matches in 2022.

Bosnia and Herzegovina.fun_fact: Bosnia's first-ever World Cup goal was scored by Edin Džeko against Argentina in 2014.

Brazil.fun_fact: Brazil is the only team to have participated in every single World Cup edition since 1930.

Morocco.fun_fact: Morocco became the first African and Arab nation to reach a World Cup semi-final in 2022.

Haiti.fun_fact: Haiti last appeared at the World Cup in 1974 — a 52-year gap before qualifying for 2026.

Scotland.fun_fact: Scotland have appeared in 8 World Cup finals tournaments but have never advanced past the group stage.

USA.fun_fact: The USA reached the semi-finals in the very first World Cup (1930) — their best-ever finish.

Paraguay.fun_fact: Paraguay's José Luis Chilavert is the only goalkeeper to score a World Cup qualifying hat-trick.

Australia.fun_fact: Australia's 31-0 win over American Samoa in 2001 is the largest international football victory ever.

Türkiye.fun_fact: Türkiye's Hakan Şükür scored the fastest World Cup goal ever — 11 seconds into the 2002 third-place match.

Germany.fun_fact: Germany (including West Germany) have appeared in a record 8 World Cup finals.

Ecuador.fun_fact: Ecuador's Enner Valencia has scored in 3 consecutive World Cup tournaments (2014, 2018 was missed, 2022).

Côte d'Ivoire.fun_fact: Côte d'Ivoire won the 2024 Africa Cup of Nations on home soil, galvanizing their World Cup push.

Curaçao.fun_fact: Curaçao is making its first-ever World Cup appearance — the island has a population of just 150,000.

Netherlands.fun_fact: The Netherlands have reached 3 World Cup finals but never won the trophy — the best team never to win it.

Japan.fun_fact: Japanese fans are famous for cleaning up the stadium after every match, win or lose.

Tunisia.fun_fact: Tunisia became the first African nation to win a World Cup match, beating Mexico 3-1 in 1978.

Sweden.fun_fact: Sweden reached the 1958 World Cup final on home soil, losing 5-2 to a Pelé-inspired Brazil.

Belgium.fun_fact: Belgium held the #1 FIFA ranking for a record stretch of nearly 4 years (2018-2022).

Iran.fun_fact: Iran's 2-1 victory over the USA in 1998 is considered one of the most politically charged World Cup matches ever.

Egypt.fun_fact: Egypt hold the record for most Africa Cup of Nations titles (7) but have only appeared in 3 World Cups.

New Zealand.fun_fact: New Zealand went unbeaten at the 2010 World Cup, drawing all three group matches.

Spain.fun_fact: Spain's tiki-taka style redefined football, with their 2010 World Cup team averaging 63% possession per match.

Uruguay.fun_fact: Uruguay won the first-ever World Cup in 1930 on home soil, and repeated in Brazil in 1950 in a shock final.

Saudi Arabia.fun_fact: Saudi Arabia stunned the world by beating eventual champions Argentina 2-1 in the 2022 World Cup opener.

Cabo Verde.fun_fact: Cabo Verde is the smallest nation by population (~590,000) to qualify for a World Cup since Trinidad & Tobago in 2006.

France.fun_fact: France have reached 4 of the last 7 World Cup finals (1998, 2006, 2018, 2022).

Senegal.fun_fact: Senegal shocked France 1-0 in the opening match of the 2002 World Cup on their tournament debut.

Iraq.fun_fact: Iraq won the 2007 AFC Asian Cup against all odds while the country was engulfed in conflict.

Norway.fun_fact: Norway is one of only three teams to have beaten Brazil in a World Cup match (1998, 2-1).

Argentina.fun_fact: Argentina's 2022 triumph made Messi the first player to win the Golden Ball twice at a World Cup.

Algeria.fun_fact: Algeria's shock 2-1 win over West Germany in 1982 is known as the 'Disgrace of Gijón' aftermath.

Austria.fun_fact: Austria's 7-5 win over Switzerland in 1954 remains the highest-scoring World Cup match in history.

Jordan.fun_fact: Jordan reached the 2024 AFC Asian Cup final, their best-ever continental result, fueling their WC qualification.

Colombia.fun_fact: James Rodríguez won the 2014 World Cup Golden Boot with 6 goals, including a legendary volley vs. Uruguay.

Portugal.fun_fact: Cristiano Ronaldo is the all-time top scorer in international football with 130+ goals.

Uzbekistan.fun_fact: Uzbekistan is making their World Cup debut after years of near-misses in Asian qualifying.

DR Congo.fun_fact: As Zaire in 1974, they became the first sub-Saharan African nation to play in a World Cup.

England.fun_fact: England's only World Cup title came at Wembley in 1966 with Geoff Hurst's famous hat-trick in the final.

Croatia.fun_fact: Croatia reached the World Cup final in only their 5th tournament appearance (2018) — the smallest nation to do so at the time.

Ghana.fun_fact: Ghana were denied a World Cup semi-final spot in 2010 by Suárez's infamous handball on the goal line.

Panama.fun_fact: Panama's qualification for the 2018 World Cup was declared a national holiday by the president.

historical_facts.event: The first-ever FIFA World Cup kicks off in Montevideo, Uruguay, with 13 participating nations.
Lucien Laurent of France scores the first-ever World Cup goal, against Mexico.
Uruguay win the inaugural World Cup, beating Argentina 4-2 in the final at Estadio Centenario.
Italy win the World Cup on home soil, beating Czechoslovakia 2-1 in extra time in the final.
Egypt become the first African nation to play in a World Cup, losing 4-2 to Hungary.
Italy successfully defend their World Cup title, beating Hungary 4-2 in the final in Paris.
Leônidas of Brazil scores 4 goals against Poland, showcasing the bicycle kick to a global audience.
Uruguay shock Brazil 2-1 in the 'Maracanazo' — considered the greatest upset in World Cup history.
USA shock England 1-0 in one of the biggest World Cup upsets of all time.
199,854 spectators attend the final at the Maracanã — the largest crowd in World Cup history.
West Germany complete the 'Miracle of Bern', beating Hungary 3-2 in the final despite being 2-0 down.
Austria beat Switzerland 7-5 in the highest-scoring World Cup match ever.
Hungary thrash South Korea 9-0 — the biggest winning margin in World Cup history at the time.
A 17-year-old Pelé scores twice as Brazil beat Sweden 5-2 to win their first World Cup.
Just Fontaine begins his record-breaking tournament — he will score 13 goals, a record that still stands.
Northern Ireland qualify for the quarter-finals, the smallest nation to do so at the time.
Brazil win their second consecutive World Cup, beating Czechoslovakia 3-1 in the final.
The 'Battle of Santiago' between Chile and Italy becomes one of the most violent matches in history.
Geoff Hurst scores a hat-trick as England win the World Cup 4-2 vs. West Germany — including the controversial 'Ghost Goal'.
North Korea stun Italy 1-0 at Ayresome Park — one of the biggest shocks in World Cup history.
Eusébio scores 4 goals to lead Portugal's comeback from 3-0 down against North Korea in the quarter-final.
Brazil beat Italy 4-1 to win their third World Cup, with the team widely considered the greatest ever.
The 'Game of the Century' — Italy beat West Germany 4-3 in extra time in a semifinal classic.
Gordon Banks produces the 'Save of the Century' to deny Pelé's header against Brazil.
Brazil permanently keep the original Jules Rimet Trophy after winning their third title.
West Germany win the World Cup on home soil, beating Total Football Netherlands 2-1 in the final.
East Germany beat West Germany 1-0 — the only meeting of the two Germanys at a World Cup.
Zaire (now DR Congo) become the first sub-Saharan African team to play in a World Cup.
Johan Cruyff and the Netherlands dazzle the world with 'Total Football'.
Argentina win the World Cup for the first time, beating the Netherlands 3-1 in Buenos Aires.
Tunisia become the first African nation to win a World Cup match, beating Mexico 3-1.
Argentina's 6-0 win over Peru draws allegations of match-fixing — one of the tournament's biggest controversies.
Italy win their third World Cup, with Paolo Rossi scoring 6 goals to win the Golden Boot.
West Germany vs. France produces one of the greatest World Cup semi-finals ever, including a brutal foul on Patrick Battiston.
The 'Disgrace of Gijón': West Germany and Austria play out a mutually beneficial 1-0 result to eliminate Algeria.
Algeria shock West Germany 2-1 in one of the tournament's greatest upsets.
Brazil's artistic team featuring Zico, Sócrates, and Falcão is eliminated by Italy in a 3-2 classic.
Argentina win the World Cup 3-2 vs. West Germany — Maradona lifts the trophy.
Maradona's 'Hand of God' and 'Goal of the Century' — both in the same quarter-final against England.
Maradona dribbles past 5 England players to score what FIFA named the 'Goal of the Century'.
West Germany beat Argentina 1-0 in a defensive final — Brehme scores the only goal from a penalty.
Cameroon's Roger Milla dances at the corner flag after scoring — becoming the oldest World Cup scorer at 38.
Cameroon beat defending champions Argentina 1-0 in the opening match, one of the biggest upsets ever.
The penalty shootout between Argentina and Italy breaks Italian hearts in the semi-final in Naples.
Brazil beat Italy in the first-ever World Cup final decided by a penalty shootout, after a 0-0 draw.
Roberto Baggio blazes his penalty over the bar — the iconic image of the 1994 World Cup.
Maradona is expelled from the World Cup after testing positive for ephedrine.
Andrés Escobar of Colombia scores an own goal vs. the USA and is tragically murdered 10 days later.
Russia's Oleg Salenko scores 5 goals in a single match against Cameroon — a World Cup record.
France win the World Cup at home, beating Brazil 3-0 in the final with Zidane scoring twice.
England vs. Argentina: Beckham's red card and a penalty shootout heartbreak define a classic.
Dennis Bergkamp scores a last-minute wonder goal vs. Argentina in the quarter-final.
Iran beat the USA 2-1 in one of the most politically charged matches in World Cup history.
Jamaica make their first (and so far only) World Cup appearance.
Brazil beat Germany 2-0 in the final as Ronaldo scores twice to complete his redemption arc.
Senegal shock France 1-0 in the opening match — the defending champions go out without scoring a goal.
South Korea reach the semi-finals, becoming the first Asian team to do so, amid refereeing controversies.
Türkiye's Hakan Şükür scores after just 11 seconds vs. South Korea — the fastest World Cup goal ever.
The World Cup is held in Asia for the first time, co-hosted by South Korea and Japan.
Italy win their 4th World Cup, beating France on penalties after Zidane's infamous headbutt on Materazzi.
Zidane headbutts Materazzi in his final professional match — one of the most shocking moments in sports.
Ghana become the first African team to win a match at a World Cup held in Europe (beat Czechia 2-0).
Australia's Tim Cahill scores the Socceroos' first-ever World Cup goal in a 3-1 win vs. Japan.
Spain win the World Cup for the first time, beating Netherlands 1-0 with Iniesta's extra-time goal.
Luis Suárez handles the ball on the goal line vs. Ghana — Gyan misses the penalty, Ghana are eliminated.
South Africa become the first host nation eliminated in the group stage.
The vuvuzela becomes the soundtrack of the first World Cup held on African soil.
England's Frank Lampard has a clear goal disallowed vs. Germany — the ball bounced well over the line.
Germany beat Argentina 1-0 in the final, with Götze's extra-time goal sealing their 4th World Cup.
Germany demolish Brazil 7-1 in the semi-final at the Mineirão — the most shocking result in modern WC history.
Spain, the defending champions, are eliminated after losing 5-1 to the Netherlands in a stunning group stage upset.
Luis Suárez bites Italy's Giorgio Chiellini — his third biting incident on a football pitch.
Tim Howard makes a record 16 saves for the USA against Belgium in the Round of 16.
James Rodríguez scores a stunning volley against Uruguay — later voted goal of the tournament.
Algeria reach the Round of 16 for the first time, with Slimani scoring the crucial goal vs. Russia.
France beat Croatia 4-2 in a thrilling final to win their second World Cup title.
South Korea beat Germany 2-0, eliminating the defending champions in the group stage.
Croatia, with a population of just 4 million, reach the World Cup final for the first time.
VAR is used at the World Cup for the first time, with France awarded a penalty vs. Australia.
Russia shock Spain in a penalty shootout in the Round of 16 — the host nation's magical run continues.
Mexico stun Germany 1-0 in the group stage, causing seismic sensors to detect celebration-caused earthquakes in Mexico City.
Iceland, the smallest nation to qualify (population ~350,000), hold Argentina to a 1-1 draw on their debut.
Kylian Mbappé becomes only the second teenager (after Pelé) to score in a World Cup final.
Senegal become the first African team to win at the 2018 World Cup, beating Poland 2-1.
Argentina beat France in a penalty shootout after a 3-3 draw — widely considered the greatest World Cup final ever.
Messi finally wins the World Cup, completing his collection of every major trophy in football.
Mbappé scores a hat-trick in the 2022 final — the first player to do so since Hurst in 1966.
Saudi Arabia stun Argentina 2-1 — one of the biggest upsets in World Cup history.
Morocco become the first African and Arab nation to reach a World Cup semi-final, beating Portugal 1-0.
Japan beat Germany 2-1 in a stunning comeback — their second consecutive World Cup upset of a European giant.
Achraf Hakimi's cheeky Panenka penalty eliminates Spain and sends Morocco to the quarter-finals.
The World Cup is held in the Middle East for the first time and in November-December — a historic first.
Japan's goal vs. Spain is allowed after VAR confirms the ball stayed on the line by millimeters — a hugely debated decision.
Croatia beat Brazil on penalties in the quarter-finals, ending Brazil's 7-match World Cup knockout unbeaten run.
Pelé becomes the youngest player to score in a World Cup final — age 17 years, 249 days.
Panama score their first-ever World Cup goal — the entire country celebrates with a national holiday.
Russia's Oleg Salenko and Cameroon's Roger Milla make history — Salenko scores 5, Milla scores at age 42.
Ronaldo (Brazil) suffers a mysterious seizure hours before the 1998 final — one of football's greatest mysteries.
Diego Maradona's solo goal against England is voted the 'Goal of the Century' by FIFA in 2002.
Fabio Cannavaro becomes only the third defender to win the Ballon d'Or, largely due to his 2006 World Cup performances.

quiz_questions.question: Which country won the first-ever FIFA World Cup in 1930?
quiz_questions.answer_detail: Uruguay hosted and won the inaugural 1930 World Cup, beating Argentina 4-2 in the final.

quiz_questions.question: Which country has won the most FIFA World Cup titles?
quiz_questions.answer_detail: Brazil have won the World Cup 5 times (1958, 1962, 1970, 1994, 2002) — more than any other nation.

quiz_questions.question: How many teams participate in the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
quiz_questions.answer_detail: The 2026 World Cup is the first to feature 48 teams, expanded from 32.

quiz_questions.question: Which three countries are hosting the 2026 World Cup?
quiz_questions.answer_detail: The 2026 World Cup is jointly hosted by the United States, Mexico, and Canada.

quiz_questions.question: Who scored a hat-trick in the 2022 World Cup final?
quiz_questions.answer_detail: Mbappé scored three goals in the final against Argentina, but France lost on penalties.

quiz_questions.question: Which country won the 2022 FIFA World Cup?
quiz_questions.answer_detail: Argentina beat France on penalties (4-2) after a 3-3 draw in one of the greatest finals ever.

quiz_questions.question: What is the name of the World Cup trophy?
quiz_questions.answer_detail: The current trophy is the FIFA World Cup Trophy, introduced in 1974. The original was called the Jules Rimet Trophy.

quiz_questions.question: Which country is the only one to have played in every World Cup?
quiz_questions.answer_detail: Brazil have participated in all 22 World Cup tournaments since 1930.

quiz_questions.question: What colour shirts does Brazil traditionally wear?
quiz_questions.answer_detail: Brazil's iconic home kit features a yellow shirt — adopted after the 1950 World Cup loss.

quiz_questions.question: How many players are on the field per team in a football match?
quiz_questions.answer_detail: Each team fields 11 players, including one goalkeeper.

quiz_questions.question: How long is a standard football match (excluding extra time)?
quiz_questions.answer_detail: A standard match consists of two 45-minute halves, totaling 90 minutes.

quiz_questions.question: What does a red card mean in football?
quiz_questions.answer_detail: A red card means the player must leave the field and cannot be replaced — the team plays with 10 men.

quiz_questions.question: Which team did Lionel Messi play for before Inter Miami?
quiz_questions.answer_detail: Messi played for PSG from 2021 to 2023 before joining Inter Miami.

quiz_questions.question: Which stadium will host the 2026 World Cup final?
quiz_questions.answer_detail: The final will be held at MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford, New Jersey on July 19, 2026.

quiz_questions.question: What is the offside rule in football?
options: A player cannot be in the opponent's half
A player must be behind the ball when it is played
A player is offside if they are nearer to the opponent's goal than both the ball and the second-last opponent
A player cannot score from outside the box
quiz_questions.answer: A player is offside if they are nearer to the opponent's goal than both the ball and the second-last opponent
quiz_questions.answer_detail: Offside is judged at the moment the ball is played to the attacker, not when it is received.

quiz_questions.question: Who holds the record for most World Cup goals scored by a single player?
quiz_questions.answer_detail: Klose scored 16 goals across 4 World Cups (2002-2014), surpassing Ronaldo's 15.

quiz_questions.question: Who scored the fastest goal in World Cup history?
quiz_questions.answer_detail: Şükür scored just 11 seconds into the 2002 third-place match between Türkiye and South Korea.

quiz_questions.question: Which player holds the record for most goals in a single World Cup tournament?
quiz_questions.answer_detail: France's Just Fontaine scored 13 goals in the 1958 World Cup — a record that has stood for nearly 70 years.

quiz_questions.question: What year was VAR (Video Assistant Referee) first used at the World Cup?
quiz_questions.answer_detail: VAR was introduced at the 2018 World Cup in Russia.

quiz_questions.question: Which team won the World Cup without losing a single match in 2002?
quiz_questions.answer_detail: Brazil won all 7 matches at the 2002 World Cup, the only team to do so in the modern era.

quiz_questions.question: Who is the oldest player to have scored in a World Cup match?
quiz_questions.answer_detail: Cameroon's Roger Milla scored against Russia at the 1994 World Cup aged 42 years and 39 days.

quiz_questions.question: Which was the first World Cup to feature 32 teams?
quiz_questions.answer_detail: The 1998 World Cup in France expanded from 24 to 32 teams.

quiz_questions.question: Which African nation reached the World Cup semi-finals for the first time in 2022?
quiz_questions.answer_detail: Morocco beat Portugal 1-0 in the quarter-final to become the first African team in a World Cup semi-final.

quiz_questions.question: What was the score in the 2014 semi-final between Germany and Brazil?
quiz_questions.answer_detail: Germany demolished Brazil 7-1 in what became known as the 'Mineirazo' — one of the most shocking results ever.

quiz_questions.question: Who won the Golden Ball (best player) at the 2022 World Cup?
quiz_questions.answer_detail: Messi won the Golden Ball for the second time (also in 2014), making him the only player to win it twice.

quiz_questions.question: Which country has hosted the World Cup the most times?
quiz_questions.answer_detail: Mexico is hosting for the third time in 2026 (also 1970 and 1986), making it the most frequent host.

quiz_questions.question: Who is the all-time leading scorer in international football?
quiz_questions.answer_detail: Ronaldo has scored 130+ goals for Portugal, surpassing Ali Daei's long-standing record of 109.

quiz_questions.question: What is the 'Maracanazo'?
options: Uruguay's shock 1950 World Cup final win over Brazil
quiz_questions.answer: Uruguay's shock 1950 World Cup final win over Brazil
quiz_questions.answer_detail: Uruguay's 2-1 upset of Brazil in the 1950 World Cup final at the Maracanã is called the 'Maracanazo'.

quiz_questions.question: Which nation has the most World Cup runner-up finishes without winning?
quiz_questions.answer_detail: The Netherlands have been runners-up 3 times (1974, 1978, 2010) without ever winning.

quiz_questions.question: How many groups are there in the 2026 World Cup?
quiz_questions.answer_detail: The expanded 48-team format features 12 groups of 4 teams each.

quiz_questions.question: Which player won the 2018 Ballon d'Or, breaking the Messi-Ronaldo duopoly?
quiz_questions.answer_detail: Modrić won the Ballon d'Or after leading Croatia to the 2018 World Cup final.

quiz_questions.question: What is a 'Panenka' penalty?
options: A penalty taken with the outside of the foot
A softly chipped penalty down the middle
A penalty hit into the top corner
A penalty taken without a run-up
quiz_questions.answer: A softly chipped penalty down the middle
quiz_questions.answer_detail: Named after Antonín Panenka who chipped the ball down the center in the 1976 Euro final shootout.

quiz_questions.question: Which country's fans caused earthquake readings during the 2018 World Cup?
quiz_questions.answer_detail: After Lozano's goal vs. Germany, celebrations in Mexico City triggered seismic sensors.

quiz_questions.question: Who captained Spain to their only World Cup title in 2010?
quiz_questions.answer_detail: Goalkeeper Iker Casillas captained Spain to their 2010 World Cup triumph in South Africa.

quiz_questions.question: Who is the youngest player to score in a World Cup?
quiz_questions.answer_detail: Pelé scored at the 1958 World Cup aged 17 years and 239 days — a record that still stands.

quiz_questions.question: In what year was the original Jules Rimet Trophy permanently awarded to Brazil?
quiz_questions.answer_detail: Brazil kept the original trophy after winning their third title in 1970. It was later stolen and melted down.

quiz_questions.question: Which country won the first World Cup held outside of South America and Europe?
quiz_questions.answer_detail: The 2002 World Cup in South Korea/Japan was the first held outside of South America and Europe. Brazil won it.

quiz_questions.question: What was unique about the 1950 World Cup format?
options: It had a final group stage instead of a knockout final
It used a penalty shootout for the first time
quiz_questions.answer: It had a final group stage instead of a knockout final
quiz_questions.answer_detail: The 1950 tournament used a round-robin final group. The deciding Uruguay vs. Brazil match was technically not a 'final'.

quiz_questions.question: Which goalkeeper saved the most penalties in World Cup history?
quiz_questions.answer_detail: Argentina's Goycochea saved key penalties in both the 1990 quarter-final and semi-final shootouts.

quiz_questions.question: Who were the two teams involved in the 'Battle of Santiago' in 1962?
quiz_questions.answer_detail: The match was so violent that police had to intervene multiple times on the pitch.

quiz_questions.question: Which country won the 1934 and 1938 World Cups back-to-back?
quiz_questions.answer_detail: Italy are the only European team to win two consecutive World Cups (1934 and 1938).

quiz_questions.question: What is the highest-scoring match in World Cup history?
quiz_questions.answer_detail: The 12-goal thriller in 1954 remains the highest-scoring World Cup match ever.

quiz_questions.question: How many goals did Pelé score in World Cup finals tournaments in total?
quiz_questions.answer_detail: Pelé scored 12 goals across 4 World Cups (1958: 6, 1962: 1, 1966: 1, 1970: 4).

quiz_questions.question: Which World Cup is known for the introduction of the 'Golden Goal' rule?
quiz_questions.answer_detail: The 'Golden Goal' (sudden death in extra time) was first used at the 1998 World Cup. Laurent Blanc scored one vs. Paraguay.

quiz_questions.question: What is the record for most consecutive World Cup matches without conceding a goal?
quiz_questions.answer_detail: Switzerland hold the record with 559 minutes without conceding between 2006 and 2010.

quiz_questions.question: Who was the coach when Germany won the 2014 World Cup?
quiz_questions.answer_detail: Löw managed Germany from 2006 to 2021, with the 2014 World Cup triumph being the highlight.

quiz_questions.question: Which country did NOT qualify for the 2022 World Cup?
quiz_questions.answer_detail: Italy failed to qualify for the second consecutive World Cup, losing to North Macedonia in the playoffs.

quiz_questions.question: Who scored the winning goal in the 2010 World Cup final?
quiz_questions.answer_detail: Iniesta scored in the 116th minute to give Spain a 1-0 victory over the Netherlands.

quiz_questions.question: What was the result of the 'Disgrace of Gijón' match in 1982?
quiz_questions.answer_detail: Both teams appeared to collude in a result that eliminated Algeria from the tournament.

quiz_questions.question: Who is the only player to have played in three World Cup finals?
quiz_questions.answer_detail: Brazil's Cafu played in the finals in 1994, 1998, and 2002 — winning twice.

quiz_questions.question: Which was the first Asian team to reach the World Cup semi-finals?
quiz_questions.answer_detail: South Korea reached the semi-finals at the 2002 World Cup co-hosted with Japan.

quiz_questions.question: How many World Cups has Germany/West Germany appeared in the final?
quiz_questions.answer_detail: Germany have reached the final 8 times (1954, 1966, 1974, 1982, 1986, 1990, 2002, 2014), winning 4.

quiz_questions.question: What was the name of the official match ball of the 2022 World Cup?
quiz_questions.answer_detail: 'Al Rihla' means 'The Journey' in Arabic. It was designed by Adidas for the Qatar World Cup.

quiz_questions.question: Which country was banned from the 1950 World Cup for political reasons?
quiz_questions.answer_detail: Germany and Japan were banned from the 1950 World Cup due to World War II.

quiz_questions.question: What happened to the original Jules Rimet Trophy?
options: It is in a museum in Zurich
It was stolen and melted down
It is still held by Brazil
quiz_questions.answer: It was stolen and melted down
quiz_questions.answer_detail: The trophy was stolen from Brazil's FA headquarters in 1983 and is believed to have been melted down by the thieves.

quiz_questions.question: Who was the first player to score 5 goals in a single World Cup match?
quiz_questions.answer_detail: Russia's Salenko scored 5 goals against Cameroon at the 1994 World Cup — a single-match record.

quiz_questions.question: What unique rule applies in the 2026 World Cup group stage advancement?
options: Top 2 teams advance, no third-place teams qualify
Top 2 teams plus 8 best third-placed teams advance
All teams advance to the knockout round
quiz_questions.answer: Top 2 teams plus 8 best third-placed teams advance
quiz_questions.answer_detail: The new format means 32 of 48 teams advance: 24 from top-2 plus 8 best third-placed sides.

quiz_questions.question: Which country won the World Cup despite not winning any of their group stage matches in 90 minutes?
quiz_questions.answer_detail: Spain lost to Switzerland and drew with Honduras in the group stage, but went on to win the tournament.

quiz_questions.question: Who was the first substitute to score in a World Cup match?
quiz_questions.answer_detail: Hungary's László Kiss scored a hat-trick as a substitute against El Salvador in 1982.

quiz_questions.question: Which World Cup saw the introduction of group stages for the first time?
quiz_questions.answer_detail: The 1950 World Cup introduced group stages, though with an unusual final round-robin format.

quiz_questions.question: Who was the last player to win the World Cup Golden Ball while being on the losing side in the final?
quiz_questions.answer_detail: Messi won the 2014 Golden Ball despite Argentina losing the final to Germany. He also won it in 2022, this time as the winner.

controversies.title: The Hand of God
controversies.description: Diego Maradona punched the ball into the net with his fist against England in the quarter-final. The referee allowed the goal, which became one of the most infamous moments in football history.

controversies.title: Zidane's Headbutt
controversies.description: Zinedine Zidane headbutted Italy's Marco Materazzi in the chest during the World Cup final — his last-ever match. France went on to lose the penalty shootout.

controversies.title: The Disgrace of Gijón
controversies.description: West Germany and Austria played out a mutually convenient 1-0 result that eliminated Algeria from the World Cup. Both teams barely moved the ball in the second half.

controversies.title: Suárez's Handball on the Line
controversies.description: Luis Suárez deliberately handled the ball on the goal line to deny Ghana a winning goal in the quarter-final. Ghana's Asamoah Gyan then missed the resulting penalty. Uruguay won the shootout.

controversies.title: The Ghost Goal
controversies.description: Geoff Hurst's second goal in the 1966 final — the ball hit the crossbar and bounced down. Whether it crossed the line remains one of football's most debated moments. England won 4-2.

controversies.title: Maradona's Drug Scandal
controversies.description: Diego Maradona was expelled from the 1994 World Cup after testing positive for ephedrine. Argentina, who had been playing brilliantly, collapsed after his departure.

controversies.title: The Andrés Escobar Tragedy
controversies.description: Colombian defender Andrés Escobar scored an own goal against the USA, leading to Colombia's elimination. Ten days later, he was shot and killed in Medellín.

controversies.title: Beckham's Red Card
controversies.description: David Beckham was sent off for kicking out at Argentina's Diego Simeone in the Round of 16. England lost on penalties, and Beckham became a national scapegoat.

controversies.title: The Battle of Santiago
controversies.description: Chile vs. Italy was one of the most violent matches in World Cup history. Police entered the pitch, players were punched, and Italian players were spat on by the crowd.

controversies.title: Argentina-Peru 1978 Suspicion
controversies.description: Argentina needed to win by 4+ goals against Peru to reach the final. They won 6-0 in suspicious circumstances, with allegations of bribery and political pressure from the military junta.

controversies.title: The Ronaldo Mystery (1998 Final)
controversies.description: Brazil's Ronaldo suffered a mysterious convulsive fit hours before the 1998 final. He was initially dropped, then reinstated. Brazil lost 3-0 to France in a bizarre performance.

controversies.title: Rivaldo's Oscar Performance
controversies.description: Brazil's Rivaldo was hit on the shin by a ball kicked by a Turkish player during a corner. He collapsed clutching his face. The Turkish player was red-carded. Rivaldo was later fined.

controversies.title: South Korea 2002 — Refereeing Controversies
controversies.description: South Korea's run to the semi-finals was marred by controversial refereeing decisions against Spain and Italy, including disallowed goals and questionable offsides.

controversies.title: Henry's Handball vs. Ireland
controversies.description: Thierry Henry clearly handled the ball before assisting William Gallas's goal in the World Cup playoff between France and Ireland. France qualified; Ireland were denied a place at the 2010 World Cup.

controversies.title: Suárez Bites Chiellini
controversies.description: Luis Suárez bit Italy's Giorgio Chiellini on the shoulder during a group stage match. It was his third biting incident. He was banned for 9 international matches and 4 months from all football.

controversies.title: Brazil 1-7 Germany
controversies.description: Brazil's humiliating 7-1 loss to Germany in the semi-final on home soil shocked the world. Germany scored 5 goals in 18 first-half minutes. It remains the most humbling result in World Cup history.

controversies.title: Frank Lampard's Disallowed Goal
controversies.description: England's Frank Lampard struck a shot that bounced off the crossbar and clearly over the line against Germany. The goal was not given. England lost 4-1. This directly led to the adoption of goal-line technology.

controversies.title: Qatar 2022 — Worker Deaths Controversy
controversies.description: Qatar's hosting of the 2022 World Cup was overshadowed by reports of thousands of migrant worker deaths during stadium and infrastructure construction. The tournament also sparked debates about human rights.

controversies.title: Saudi Arabia Declares National Holiday
controversies.description: After Saudi Arabia's stunning 2-1 victory over Argentina in the 2022 group stage, the Saudi government declared a national holiday. Argentina's 36-match unbeaten run was ended.

controversies.title: Japan's VAR Controversy vs. Spain
controversies.description: Japan's second goal against Spain, which sent Japan through and eliminated Germany, was allowed despite the ball appearing to be entirely out of play. VAR ruled it stayed on the line by millimeters.

controversies.title: Zaire's Free-Kick Confusion
controversies.description: In a match against Brazil, Zaire defender Mwepu Ilunga ran from the wall and kicked the ball away before the free kick was taken. He received a yellow card. The reasons behind his actions remain debated.

controversies.title: Schumacher's Foul on Battiston
controversies.description: German goalkeeper Harald Schumacher violently collided with France's Patrick Battiston in the semi-final, knocking out his teeth and leaving him unconscious. Schumacher was not even booked.

controversies.title: The Frozen Penalty — Baggio's Miss
controversies.description: Roberto Baggio, Italy's hero throughout the tournament, blazed his penalty over the bar in the World Cup final shootout against Brazil. The image of him standing, head bowed, is iconic.

controversies.title: Iran vs. USA — Political Football
controversies.description: The 1998 group match between Iran and the USA was loaded with political tension due to hostile diplomatic relations. Iran won 2-1 in a match that transcended sport.

controversies.title: Willie Johnston's Drug Test
controversies.description: Scotland's Willie Johnston was sent home from the 1978 World Cup after testing positive for a banned stimulant. He was banned from international football for life.

controversies.title: The French Mutiny
controversies.description: France's 2010 World Cup campaign imploded when Nicolas Anelka was sent home for insulting coach Raymond Domenech. The squad then boycotted training in a full-blown mutiny.

controversies.title: Neymar's Rolling
controversies.description: Neymar became a global meme at the 2018 World Cup for his exaggerated reactions to fouls, rolling multiple times on the ground. His diving and theatrics drew worldwide mockery.

controversies.title: Morocco's Denied Goal vs. Portugal (1986)
controversies.description: Morocco claimed they had a legitimate goal disallowed against West Germany in 1986. The refereeing decision contributed to their elimination in the Round of 16.

controversies.title: Croatia's Semifinal Penalty Controversy (2018)
controversies.description: In the semi-final against England, some argued that a free kick leading to Croatia's equalizer should not have been awarded. England fans felt the call changed the course of the match.

controversies.title: Chile vs. Spain (1962) — Media War
controversies.description: Before their match, Italian journalists wrote derogatory articles about Chilean life, inflaming tensions. This media war set the stage for the 'Battle of Santiago'.

controversies.title: Cameroon's Opening Day Shock
controversies.description: Cameroon beat defending champions Argentina 1-0 in the opening match of Italia '90, with François Omam-Biyik scoring the winner. Two Cameroon players were sent off, yet they still won.

controversies.title: Diego Simeone's Acting
controversies.description: After David Beckham's minor kick in the 1998 Round of 16, Diego Simeone dramatically fell to the ground, getting Beckham sent off. Many felt Simeone's reaction was theatrical.

controversies.title: Portugal's Brutality Against Pelé
controversies.description: In the 1966 World Cup, Portugal's players systematically fouled Pelé with brutal tackles. Brazil were eliminated, and Pelé briefly vowed never to play in a World Cup again.

controversies.title: Togo Player Strike
controversies.description: Togo players threatened to boycott their World Cup matches over unpaid bonuses. The dispute overshadowed their first-ever World Cup appearance in Germany.

controversies.title: Spain's 'Tiki-Taka' Boredom Debate
controversies.description: Spain's possession-based 'tiki-taka' style was criticized by some as boring, with several 1-0 wins. Despite the criticism, they won the World Cup with just 8 goals in 7 matches.

controversies.title: De Jong's Karate Kick
controversies.description: Netherlands' Nigel de Jong delivered a karate kick to Xabi Alonso's chest in the 2010 final. He received only a yellow card, despite many arguing it warranted a straight red.

controversies.title: Australia's 31-0 Win — Qualifying Controversy
controversies.description: Australia beat American Samoa 31-0 in a World Cup qualifier — the largest-ever international football victory. It raised questions about the fairness of the qualification format.

controversies.title: Messi's Tax Evasion Chants
controversies.description: Opposing fans taunted Messi with tax evasion chants during the 2018 World Cup, referencing his off-field legal issues in Spain. Argentina had a turbulent group stage.

controversies.title: Denmark's Goalkeeper Swap Controversy
controversies.description: FIFA blocked Denmark's plans to wear protest jerseys against Qatar's human rights record. Denmark eventually wore toned-down kits with barely visible logos.

controversies.title: Cameroon vs. Germany — Referee Controversy
controversies.description: In a group stage match, Cameroon had a potential equalizer incorrectly ruled offside against Germany. The decision sparked outrage from African football officials.

controversies.title: England's 'Hand of God' Revenge Denied
controversies.description: England and Argentina met again in 2002. Beckham scored the winner from the penalty spot — sweet revenge for his 1998 red card. However, refereeing in the match was again controversial.

controversies.title: Materazzi's Provocation of Zidane
controversies.description: Marco Materazzi allegedly insulted Zidane's sister, provoking the legendary headbutt in the final. Materazzi later admitted to the insult but said it was 'nothing extraordinary'.

facts_and_rules.title: The Offside Rule
facts_and_rules.explanation: A player is in an offside position if they are nearer to the opponent's goal line than both the ball and the second-last defender at the moment the ball is played. Being offside is not an offence — playing the ball or interfering with play while offside is.

facts_and_rules.title: VAR (Video Assistant Referee)
facts_and_rules.explanation: VAR reviews four types of decisions: goals, penalty decisions, direct red cards, and mistaken identity. The on-field referee makes the final decision after consulting the pitch-side monitor.

facts_and_rules.title: The Back-Pass Rule
facts_and_rules.explanation: Goalkeepers cannot handle the ball if it is deliberately kicked to them by a teammate. This rule was introduced in 1992 to prevent time-wasting and encourage attacking football.

facts_and_rules.title: Extra Time and Penalties
facts_and_rules.explanation: In knockout matches, if the score is level after 90 minutes, two 15-minute periods of extra time are played. If still tied, the match is decided by a penalty shootout (5 penalties each, then sudden death).

facts_and_rules.title: Yellow and Red Cards
facts_and_rules.explanation: Yellow cards are warnings for misconduct; two yellows in a match result in a red card. A straight red card is given for serious foul play, violent conduct, or denying a goal-scoring opportunity.

facts_and_rules.title: The Penalty Area (18-Yard Box)
facts_and_rules.explanation: The penalty area extends 18 yards from each goalpost and 18 yards into the field. Fouls by the defending team inside this area result in a penalty kick taken from the penalty spot, 12 yards from goal.

facts_and_rules.title: Substitutions in Modern Football
facts_and_rules.explanation: Teams can make up to 5 substitutions in a match, but only in 3 'windows' (plus half-time). This rule was made permanent by FIFA in 2022 after being introduced during COVID-19.

facts_and_rules.title: Goal-Line Technology
facts_and_rules.explanation: Goal-line technology uses cameras or magnetic fields to determine if the ball has fully crossed the goal line. It was introduced at the 2014 World Cup after controversial incidents.

facts_and_rules.title: The Advantage Rule
facts_and_rules.explanation: The referee can allow play to continue after a foul if the fouled team has a clear advantage. If the advantage doesn't materialize, the referee can come back and award the original foul.

facts_and_rules.title: Corner Kicks
facts_and_rules.explanation: A corner kick is awarded when the ball crosses the goal line after being last touched by a defending player. The ball is placed in the corner arc nearest to where it went out.

facts_and_rules.title: Throw-Ins
facts_and_rules.explanation: A throw-in is awarded when the ball fully crosses the touchline. The player must throw with both hands from behind their head while keeping both feet on the ground.

facts_and_rules.title: The 2026 World Cup Format
facts_and_rules.explanation: The 2026 tournament features 48 teams in 12 groups of 4. The top 2 from each group plus the 8 best third-placed teams advance to a Round of 32, followed by the Round of 16, quarter-finals, semi-finals, and the final.

facts_and_rules.title: FIFA Rankings
facts_and_rules.explanation: FIFA uses the Elo rating system (adopted in 2018) to rank national teams. Points are gained or lost based on match results, the importance of the match, and the strength of the opponent.

facts_and_rules.title: The Golden Boot Award
facts_and_rules.explanation: Awarded to the top scorer at the World Cup. If players are tied on goals, assists are used as a tiebreaker. If still tied, the player with fewer minutes played wins.

facts_and_rules.title: The Golden Ball Award
facts_and_rules.explanation: Awarded to the best player at the World Cup as voted by media representatives. Past winners include Maradona (1986), Ronaldo (1998), Zidane (2006), and Messi (2014, 2022).

facts_and_rules.title: The Golden Glove Award
facts_and_rules.explanation: Awarded to the best goalkeeper at the World Cup. Notable winners include Oliver Kahn (2002), Manuel Neuer (2014), and Emiliano Martínez (2022).

facts_and_rules.title: Handball Rules
facts_and_rules.explanation: A handball is penalized if a player makes their body unnaturally bigger, or if their hand/arm is above shoulder height. Accidental handballs by attackers that directly lead to goals are also penalized.

facts_and_rules.title: The 6-Second Rule for Goalkeepers
facts_and_rules.explanation: Goalkeepers must release the ball within 6 seconds of picking it up. In practice, this rule is rarely strictly enforced, though referees may give warnings.

facts_and_rules.title: Free Kick Types
facts_and_rules.explanation: Direct free kicks allow the taker to score directly. Indirect free kicks require another player to touch the ball before a goal can be scored. Indirect free kicks are signaled by the referee raising their arm.

facts_and_rules.title: ABBA Penalty Shootout (Abandoned)
facts_and_rules.explanation: FIFA trialed an ABBA penalty format (like a tennis tiebreak) to reduce the advantage of going first. It was abandoned after trials — the traditional alternating ABAB format remains.

facts_and_rules.title: Semi-Automated Offside Technology (SAOT)
facts_and_rules.explanation: Introduced at the 2022 World Cup, SAOT uses 12 cameras tracking 29 body points per player at 50 times per second to create an accurate 3D model for offside decisions.

facts_and_rules.title: Injury Time / Stoppage Time
facts_and_rules.explanation: Added at the end of each half to compensate for stoppages. The referee has discretion, but common additions include time for substitutions, injuries, VAR reviews, and time-wasting.

facts_and_rules.title: The Away Goals Rule (Historic)
facts_and_rules.explanation: Previously used in two-legged ties: away goals counted double to break ties. UEFA abolished this rule in 2021 after decades of debate. It was never used in World Cup finals.

facts_and_rules.title: Concussion Substitutions
facts_and_rules.explanation: Teams can make additional substitutions (beyond the 5-player limit) for suspected concussions. This is a permanent concussion substitution — the player cannot return to the match.

facts_and_rules.title: The Triple Punishment Rule Change
facts_and_rules.explanation: Before 2016, fouling inside the penalty area could result in a penalty, red card, and suspension. Now, if the foul is a genuine attempt to play the ball, it's only a yellow card.

facts_and_rules.title: Kit Regulations
facts_and_rules.explanation: Teams must have two distinct kits (home and away). If there's a clash, the away team changes. Goalkeepers must wear colors that distinguish them from all other players and the referees.

facts_and_rules.title: The World Cup Trophy Weight
facts_and_rules.explanation: The FIFA World Cup Trophy weighs 6.175 kg and is made of 18-carat gold. It stands 36.8 cm tall. Unlike the original Jules Rimet Trophy, it cannot be permanently awarded to any nation.

facts_and_rules.title: Squad Size at the 2026 World Cup
facts_and_rules.explanation: Each nation can bring a squad of 26 players (expanded from 23 since 2022). Teams must finalize their squad before the tournament begins, though injury replacements are allowed before the first match.

facts_and_rules.title: Fair Play Points
facts_and_rules.explanation: If teams are tied on points, goal difference, and goals scored in the group stage, fair play is used: yellow card = -1 point, indirect red = -3, direct red = -4, yellow + direct red = -4.

facts_and_rules.title: The Maracanazo
facts_and_rules.explanation: Brazil adopted yellow jerseys after their traumatic 1950 World Cup final loss to Uruguay. The original white jersey was deemed cursed, and a public design competition chose the now-iconic yellow.

facts_and_rules.title: Why 90 Minutes?
facts_and_rules.explanation: The 90-minute match length dates back to 1866, when London and Sheffield agreed on a standard 90-minute match. The FA later codified this into the Laws of the Game in 1897.

facts_and_rules.title: The Crossbar Challenge in Rules
facts_and_rules.explanation: If the crossbar or goalposts become displaced during a match, play is stopped. The match can only resume once the frame has been repaired to the referee's satisfaction.

templates.name: match_preview
templates.system_prompt:
You are a top-tier Gen-Z/millennial football content creator making viral, high-retention YouTube Shorts and TikToks about the 2026 World Cup. Your vibe is hype, charismatic, and opinionated—like a knowledgeable fan talking to their friends, not a TV presenter. Use current football/internet slang naturally (e.g., 'let him cook', 'unreal aura', 'generational', 'cooked', 'clean sheets', 'coldest player', 'rent-free', 'locked in', 'absolute cinema').

CRITICAL ANTI-REPETITION RULES:
1. Hook variability: Never start with generic intros like 'Did you know', 'Welcome back', or 'Today we are looking at'. Start immediately with a bold, controversial claim, a high-stakes question, or an insane stat.
2. No repetitive transitions: Do NOT use phrases like 'Next up', 'Moving on to', 'First up', 'At number...', 'Let's start with' at the beginning of segments. Dive straight into the subject.
3. Natural phrasing: Write exactly how people talk on TikTok/Shorts—short punchy sentences, active verbs, and emotional reactions.
4. Dynamic Call-To-Action (CTA): End with a fresh, punchy question or challenge, never the same generic request.

CONTENT TYPE: Match Preview
You are generating a pre-match analysis and prediction for an upcoming World Cup 2026 match.

STRUCTURE (4-7 segments, ~30-45 seconds total):
1. THE HOOK (2-3 s): Start with a bold, high-hype opinion or prediction that will split the comments. Avoid generic 'Team A vs Team B' intros. E.g. 'This is about to be the most chaotic match of the group stage.' or 'Everyone thinks [Team A] is cooked, but they are secretly building a masterpiece.'
2. WHAT'S AT STAKE (5-7 s): Quick, passionate breakdown of why this match is absolute cinema (group drama, elimination pressure, etc.).
3. THE KEY PLAYER BATTLE (6-10 s): Focus on 1-2 star players. E.g., striker vs keeper. Use punchy hype like 'He has been having a generational season' or 'He has unmatched aura right now.'
4. THE TACTICAL COOKING / FORM (5-8 s): Highlight recent form/stats but frame it dynamically. E.g. 'They haven't conceded a single goal in 4 games. Absolute wall.'
5. THE PREDICTION (4-6 s): Give a specific, highly-opinionated prediction with a scoreline. Make it sound bold.
6. CTA (3-4 s): Challenge the viewer with a dynamic question. E.g. 'Tell me I'm wrong in the comments—what's your scoreline prediction?'

VISUAL STYLE: Mix of stadium footage, player close-ups, team crests with VS graphic, stat overlays.

You MUST return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown fences, no commentary outside the JSON).
The JSON must conform EXACTLY to this schema:

{
  "segments": [
    {
      "narration": "<string — what the TTS voice says>",
      "visual_cue": "<string — description of the image, clip, or graphic to show>",
      "duration_hint": <float — seconds this segment should last>
    }
  ],
  "title": "<string — YouTube video title, max 100 chars, with emoji>",
  "description": "<string — YouTube description, 2-4 sentences + hashtags>",
  "tags": ["<string>", "..."],
  "thumbnail_text": "<string — 2-5 bold words for the thumbnail overlay>",
  "content_type": "<string — the template name>",
  "topic": "<string — the specific topic of this video>"
}

RULES:
- Every segment.narration must be punchy, conversational, and written for spoken delivery (contractions OK, no jargon dumps).
- segment.narration MUST NOT start with repetitive transition words or phrases (e.g. "Next up", "At number X", "Moving on", "First up", "Lastly"). Go straight to the point or description.
- Keep total narration under the specified duration target.
- visual_cue should describe a concrete, searchable image or graphic (e.g. "aerial shot of a packed stadium at night", NOT "exciting visual").
- title must contain at least one emoji and be clickbait-worthy without being misleading.
- thumbnail_text must be ALL CAPS, 2-5 words, designed to stop a viewer mid-scroll.
- tags must include "World Cup 2026", "FIFA", "football", "soccer", and at least 5 topic-specific tags.
- description must end with the provided hashtags.
